title: Kal Bhairav temple
tagline: Kal Bhairav temple — spiritual town of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Kal Bhairav temple is a Hindu temple located in the Ujjain city of Madhya Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Kal Bhairav temple is a Hindu temple located in the Ujjain city of Madhya Pradesh, India. It is dedicated to Kal Bhairav, the guardian deity of the city. Located on the banks of the Shipra River, it is one of the most active temples in the city, visited by hundreds of devotees daily. Liquor is one of the offerings made to the temple deity.

nearestAirport.name: Devi Ahilya Bai Holkar Airport, Indore

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Siddhwat Temple
topPlaces.description: Siddhwat temple (also spelled Siddhavat) is located at Bhairavgarh, Ujjain (MP), India at the banks of Shipra river. This temple is also known as Shaktibhed shrine.

topPlaces.name: Kaliadeh Palace
topPlaces.description: Kaliadeh Palace is a palace on the banks of the Shipra River in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh. It was built by the Sultan of Mandu in 1458 AD during the time of Mahmud Khilji.

topPlaces.name: Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga
topPlaces.description: Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga (IAST: mahākāleśvara) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva and is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas, shrines which are said to be the most sacred abodes of Shiva. It is located in the ancient city of Ujjain in the state of Madhya Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Vikramaditya Statue
topPlaces.description: The Vikramaditya Statue is a 9.1-metre (30-foot) statue of King Vikramaditya at Vikram Teela behind Mahakal Temple in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, India. The Statue is made of brass with a cost of ₹1 crore (US$100,000) by Ujjain Municipal Corporation and 'Simhastha Preparation Committee'.

topPlaces.name: Dewas Gate Inter State Bus Stand
topPlaces.description: Shaheed Raja Bhau Mahakal Bus Stand or Dewas Gate Inter State Bus Stand is a bus terminus in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh. Dewas Gate is a bus terminus that provides bus service to destinations located in other states.

image.alt: Dewas Gate Inter State Bus Stand

topPlaces.name: Malwa Subah
topPlaces.description: The Malwa Subah (Persian: صوبه ملوا) was one of the original twelve Subahs (provinces) of the Mughal Empire, including Gondwana, from 1568–1743. Its seat was Ujjain in the Malwa region.

topPlaces.name: Freeganj
topPlaces.description: Freeganj is the most affluent and expensive area of Ujjain, the fourth largest city in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. Freeganj is known for its education hub.

topPlaces.name: Jantar Mantar, Ujjain
topPlaces.description: Jantar Mantar or Vedh Shala in New Ujjain is an observatory designed to be used with the naked eye. It is one of five Jantar Mantar in India.

items.note: Siddhwat temple (also spelled Siddhavat) is located at Bhairavgarh, Ujjain (MP), India at the banks of Shipra river. This temple is also known as Shaktibhed shrine.
Kaliadeh Palace is a palace on the banks of the Shipra River in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh. It was built by the Sultan of Mandu in 1458 AD during the time of Mahmud Khilji.

Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga (IAST: mahākāleśvara) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva and is one of the twelve Jyotirlingas, shrines which are said to be the most sacred abodes of Shiva. It is located in the ancient city of Ujjain in the state of Madhya Pradesh, India.

The Vikramaditya Statue is a 9.1-metre (30-foot) statue of King Vikramaditya at Vikram Teela behind Mahakal Temple in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, India. The Statue is made of brass with a cost of ₹1 crore (US$100,000) by Ujjain Municipal Corporation and 'Simhastha Preparation Committee'.

items.activity: Dewas Gate Inter State Bus Stand
items.note: Shaheed Raja Bhau Mahakal Bus Stand or Dewas Gate Inter State Bus Stand is a bus terminus in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh. Dewas Gate is a bus terminus that provides bus service to destinations located in other states.
The Malwa Subah (Persian: صوبه ملوا) was one of the original twelve Subahs (provinces) of the Mughal Empire, including Gondwana, from 1568–1743. Its seat was Ujjain in the Malwa region.
Freeganj is the most affluent and expensive area of Ujjain, the fourth largest city in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. Freeganj is known for its education hub.
Jantar Mantar or Vedh Shala in New Ujjain is an observatory designed to be used with the naked eye. It is one of five Jantar Mantar in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kal Bhairav temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kal Bhairav temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kal Bhairav temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Devi Ahilya Bai Holkar Airport, Indore (55 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhopal Junction (167 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kal Bhairav temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kal Bhairav temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kal Bhairav temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kal Bhairav temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kal Bhairav temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siddhwat Temple, Kaliadeh Palace, Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga, Vikramaditya Statue, Dewas Gate Inter State Bus Stand.
